prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark, nearly black frame in which no clear objects or people are visible.

It then cuts to a soft-focus, layered image. A woman is visible seated indoors, wearing a light-colored top. The image appears partially superimposed with a close-up of an object that looks like a wrist or bracelet area, creating a reflective or double-exposure effect. The setting is indistinct, with light coming from the left and darker vertical shapes behind her.

Next, another soft, hazy shot shows a woman seated near a bright window or light source. She appears to be wearing a darker top and is positioned behind or beside a reflective surface, possibly glass. The scene remains visually blurred and atmospheric, with the woman’s face and upper body visible but details unclear.

The commercial then cuts to a close-up of a wrist. A hand is fastening or adjusting a metal bracelet or watchband around the wrist. The cuff of a light shirt sleeve is visible, along with darker clothing beyond it. This is the clearest product-use shot in the sampled frames.

The final frame is a black product title card. White text reads “Lady Romunda” with “Speidel” below it in a script-like font. Beneath the text is a sparkling metal bracelet or watchband displayed horizontally, with bright star-like highlights emphasizing its shine. A small line of text appears in the lower right, but it is too small and unclear to read confidently.
